The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sannari Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Sannari Kofun]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sannari Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sannari Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Sannari Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sannari Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji ware]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun and historical site located in the San'yō region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sannari Kofun | native_name =三成古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sannari Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E|region:JP-31|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th to early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|04|03|N|133|53|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sannari Kofun'''|三成古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakakitashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211090|title= 三成古墳|trans-title= Sannari Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sannari Kofun is located on the left bank of the Kume River, which flows to the west of the Tsuyama Basin, at the edge of a south-facing hill. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) with a length of 35 meters, and orientated to the east. While the north face of the hillside has been shaped only to the extent that it is difficult to distinguish it from the natural topography, the south and west faces facing the Tsuyama Basin are clearly intentionally shaped. In 1977, when the landowner (a Buddhist temple) began to clear the land for the construction of a cemetery, a box-type stone [[sarcophagus]] was discovered. Up until this point, it had been unknown that this was an ancient Kofun, and the construction work leveled much of the north side of the tumulus. The south side has relatively preserved its original shape, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' was confirmed in the posterior portion. In 1978, an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Okayama Prefectural Board of Education.
Box-type sarcophagi made with three flagstones for each side wall and one stone plate for each edge, and three lid stones were found in both the anterior and posterior portions of the tumulus, and are aligned with the main axis of the tumulus. In addition, three more small box-type sarcophaguses, believed to be for infants, were excavated near the rear of the tumulus.
The inside of the first large sarcophagus measured 190 cm long, 45 cm wide and 30 cm deep on average. Inside were two sets of human remains placed facing each other, crossing each other near the thighs. Based on the state of burial, one of the sets of bones appears to have been a subsequent burial. Only the lower set of bones was accompanied by a pillow stone. The human bones were painted [[vermilion]], and the inside of the sarcophagus was also painted with red pigment. The second main burial was also that of a pair of remains, very similar to the first set: however, only the first burial was accompanied by [[grave goods]]. These included an iron sword, an iron axe, and a ''[[magatama]]'' as well as a [[bronze mirror]] depicting four beasts and seven pseudo inscriptions. Based on the burial goods and excavated shards of [[Haji pottery]] pottery, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Restoration work was carried out and the tumulus has regained its original appearance. The excavated artifacts are stored and exhibited at the {{nihongo|Tsuyama City Kume Museum of History and Folklore|津山市久米歴史民俗資料館||}}. The tumulus is located about six minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]] [[Mimasaka-Sendai Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
